summary: DHH muses about his inability to remember dreams, noting that while others report vivid nightmares and wild dream adventures, he rarely recalls any dreams at all. He laments that eight hours of sleep each night yields no memorable mental experiences, wishing for occasional nocturnal adventures.
key_points: DHH can count his lifetime nightmares on one hand and hasn't remembered a dream in months
He references a 'nightmare project' website where people catalog their vivid dream experiences
He frames dreamless sleep as wasted potential for mental adventure
money_quotes: Eight hours are spend every night sleeping and I've got nothing to show for it.
Is it too much to ask for to get a few adventures of the mind just once in a while?
I can count the number of nightmares I've ever had on a single hand.
key_insight: A lighthearted early blog post where DHH wishes his sleeping hours would at least produce memorable dreams rather than yielding nothing at all.
